task_completion.description: Import the survey data from site_survey.csv into TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal interface showing imported survey points in the workspace, or an import dialog confirming site_survey.csv was successfully loaded with 300 points visible.

task_completion.description: Build a triangulated terrain model (TIN/MDT) from the imported survey points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal workspace displaying a generated triangulated irregular network (TIN) or terrain mesh connecting the survey points.

task_completion.description: Use TopoCal's volume computation tool (Volumen con plano / cota) to test multiple candidate grading elevations, systematically narrowing toward a balanced cut/fill ratio.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiple screenshots showing the TopoCal volume computation dialog with different cota (elevation) values entered across different steps, and volume results (Corte/Relleno or Cut/Fill numbers) visible in the results display. At least 5 distinct elevation values should be visible across the trajectory.

task_completion.description: Create grading_report.txt documenting each elevation tested with its cut and fill volumes, and the final balanced elevation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (Notepad or similar) showing grading_report.txt open with multiple lines containing elevation values paired with cut and fill volume numbers. The file should show at least 5 elevation entries and a clearly identified final/optimal elevation.

task_completion.description: Generate contour lines at 2-meter intervals on the terrain model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal workspace showing topographic contour lines drawn across the terrain model, or a contour generation dialog with a 2m interval setting.

task_completion.description: Export the terrain with contour lines to site_grading.dxf in the GradingStudy folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Export dialog in TopoCal saving 'site_grading.dxf' to C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\GradingStudy\, or a file explorer window confirming the DXF file exists at that location.

task_completion.description: Save the TopoCal project as site_grading.top in the GradingStudy folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Save dialog in TopoCal saving the project as 'site_grading.top', or file explorer confirming the file exists in the GradingStudy folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the TopoCal graphical user interface to perform terrain modeling and volume computations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively clicking through TopoCal menus (MDT, Cálculos, Curvas de nivel), toolbars, and dialog boxes rather than using terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Volume results were genuinely computed by TopoCal, not fabricated.
integrity.visual_evidence: TopoCal's volume computation dialog or results window is visible with numeric cut/fill values that change across different elevation tests, showing real computation rather than made-up numbers.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually write DXF content in a text editor to fake the deliverable.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors are shown editing raw DXF file contents. The DXF was exported through TopoCal's export functionality.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The survey data contains 300 USGS NED 10m elevation points for El Paso County, Colorado Springs area. The elevation range is approximately 1850-2100m. The agent must use TopoCal's 'Volumen con plano' tool (accessible via MDT or Cálculos menu) to test different 'cota' (elevation) values. The balanced elevation where cut equals fill will be somewhere within the data's elevation range. The agent should test at least 5 elevations and the final cut-to-fill ratio should be between 0.9 and 1.1. The report must show the iterative search process, not just a final answer.
